commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=e751b5f388d6fde9dbfcf521d42bb4a955170ffb)
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds a markdown helper, wires local memory-wiki ingest to preserve an existing human Notes block on re-ingest, and adds a focused regression test.

PR surface: Source +20, Tests +41. Total +61 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main writes a regenerated source page with an empty Notes block, and the PR body includes before/after live output from a real on-disk vault. I did not run repository tests in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A narrow helper and regression-test repair can address the marker-in-content blocker without product judgment.

target.title: fix(memory-wiki): preserve human notes block on source re-ingest
target.body:
## Summary

Re-ingesting an already-ingested memory-wiki source destroys the user's notes. `openclaw wiki ingest <path>` (and the gateway `wiki` ingest tool) write each source page with a `## Notes` block delimited by `<!-- openclaw:human:start -->` / `<!-- openclaw:human:end -->` markers that the user is meant to edit. When the same source is ingested again (same title, so same slug and same `sources/<slug>.md` path), the page is regenerated with an empty Notes block and overwritten, silently dropping anything the user wrote inside the human-managed block.

This contradicts the documented contract in `docs/plugins/memory-wiki.md:129`: "Managed content stays inside generated blocks. Human note blocks are preserved."

## Root cause

`extensions/memory-wiki/src/ingest.ts` rebuilds the full page body with a hardcoded empty human block and writes it unconditionally, never reading the existing page:

```ts
// before
      "## Notes",
      "<!-- openclaw:human:start -->",
      "<!-- openclaw:human:end -->",
      "",
    ].join("\n"),
  });

  await fs.writeFile(pagePath, markdown, "utf8");
```

The sibling write paths already preserve the human block by reading the existing page first: synthesis (`apply.ts` `buildSynthesisBody` threads `originalBody` through `replaceManagedMarkdownBlock`) and the ChatGPT importer (`chatgpt-import.ts` `preserveExistingPageBlocks` re-injects the existing human block). Ingest was the lone writer that skipped this step.

## Fix

When the page already exists, read it and re-inject its existing human Notes block into the freshly rendered markdown before writing. A small shared helper lives next to the existing human-block markers in `markdown.ts`:

```ts
// after - markdown.ts
const HUMAN_NOTES_BLOCK_PATTERN = new RegExp(`${HUMAN_START_MARKER}[\\s\\S]*?${HUMAN_END_MARKER}`);

export function preserveHumanNotesBlock(rendered: string, existing: string): string {
  const existingBlock = existing.match(HUMAN_NOTES_BLOCK_PATTERN)?.[0];
  if (!existingBlock) {
    return rendered;
  }
  return rendered.replace(HUMAN_NOTES_BLOCK_PATTERN, () => existingBlock);
}
```

```ts
// after - ingest.ts
  const existing = created ? "" : await fs.readFile(pagePath, "utf8").catch(() => "");
  await fs.writeFile(
    pagePath,
    existing ? preserveHumanNotesBlock(markdown, existing) : markdown,
    "utf8",
  );
```

The function-form `replace` callback returns the captured block verbatim, so user notes containing `$&`, `$1`, etc. are preserved literally rather than being interpreted as replacement tokens. New page creation is unchanged (`created` short-circuits the read), and the freshly generated source content is still written; only the human block is carried forward.

## Why this is the right boundary

`ingestMemoryWikiSource` is the single function behind both the `wiki ingest` CLI command (`cli.ts:588`) and the gateway ingest tool, so fixing it covers both entry points. The human-block markers and detection already live in `markdown.ts`, so the preserve helper is colocated with the contract it protects. The two sibling writers (synthesis, ChatGPT import) already preserve the block, so no other surface needs changing; this change brings ingest in line with them.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-wiki/src/ingest-human-notes.test.ts` passes with the patch and fails on pristine main (the note is gone after re-ingest).
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs` and `oxfmt --check` clean on the three changed files.
- tsgo core typecheck clean.
- Existing `ingest.test.ts` create-path assertion still passes unchanged.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: re-ingesting the same memory-wiki source wiped user notes stored in the page's human-managed `## Notes` block; with the patch the notes survive while the regenerated source content is still updated.
Real environment tested: drove the real `ingestMemoryWikiSource` runtime function (the exact function behind `wiki ingest` and the gateway ingest tool) against a real on-disk vault on pristine main `a39e548ede` and then on the patched tree, with identical inputs: ingest a file, add a note inside the human markers, update the source, re-ingest. Everything stayed real (real vault on disk, real render/write path); nothing was stubbed.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: ingest `roadmap.txt` (v1), edit `sources/roadmap.md` adding a note inside the human block, overwrite `roadmap.txt` with v2, re-ingest the same path, then read the page back from disk.
Evidence after fix:
```
# BEFORE (pristine main a39e548ede)
new source content present: true
user note present after re-ingest: false
--- on-disk Notes block ---
## Notes
<!-- openclaw:human:start -->
<!-- openclaw:human:end -->

# AFTER (this patch, identical inputs)
new source content present: true
user note present after re-ingest: true
--- on-disk Notes block ---
## Notes
<!-- openclaw:human:start -->
KEY INSIGHT: covers $1 of the Q2 roadmap
<!-- openclaw:human:end -->
```
Observed result after fix: the re-ingested page keeps the user's note (including the literal `$1`) inside the human block while still showing the updated v2 source content; on pristine main the same note is gone.
What was not tested: no live network or provider request is involved in ingest; full repo build not run.
